message:
fix(fnx-algorithms): implement bellman-ford all_shortest_paths [br-r37-c1-xsi7c]

Closes parity gap where fnx.all_shortest_paths(DiGraph, weight=...,
method='bellman-ford') raised PyNotImplementedError. NetworkX uses
bellman_ford_predecessor_and_distance with multi-pred lists; the existing
fnx bellman_ford_shortest_paths_directed only stored a single Option<String>
predecessor and so could not enumerate equal-weight shortest paths.

Adds two new functions in fnx-algorithms:
- all_shortest_paths_weighted_bellman_ford (undirected)
- all_shortest_paths_weighted_directed_bellman_ford

Both run full bellman-ford then build a multi-predecessor map from final
distances and reuse the existing build_all_paths_from_preds DFS for path
enumeration. Returns Result<Vec<Vec<String>>, ()> where Err signals a
negative cycle (caller maps to NetworkXUnbounded).

Wires the bellman-ford branch in fnx-python's all_shortest_paths through
these new functions for both undirected and directed graphs (the previous
undirected branch was also a NotImplementedError, but worked for positive
weights in NX). Negative-weight undirected edges still raise
NetworkXUnbounded — implicit u-v-u cycle of weight 2w<0 — matching NX.

Live parity check (verified):
- Directed diamond, positive weights: identical paths to NX dijkstra/bf
- Directed graph with negative cycle: both raise NetworkXUnbounded
- Undirected diamond, positive weights: identical paths to NX bf

Adds 5 Rust unit tests covering diamond/unreachable/negative-cycle cases
for both directed and undirected variants.
